title: MATERIALIZED CTE with FALSE condition causes incorrect empty result in multi-table JOIN
description:
### What happens?

When using a MATERIALIZED CTE with a FALSE condition combined with multi-table JOINs, DuckDB returns an empty result set when it should return rows. The same query without the MATERIALIZED keyword or without the CTE works correctly.

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c INT);
CREATE TABLE t2(c INT);
INSERT INTO t0 VALUES (1);
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (1);
INSERT INTO t2 VALUES (1);

WITH foo AS MATERIALIZED (
  SELECT 1 AS one
  FROM t0
  WHERE FALSE
)
SELECT t0.c
FROM t0
JOIN t1 ON TRUE
JOIN t2 ON TRUE;
-- empty set

WITH foo AS MATERIALIZED (
  SELECT 1 AS one
  FROM t0
  WHERE FALSE
)
SELECT t0.c
FROM t0
JOIN t1 ON NOT EXISTS (SELECT one FROM foo)
JOIN t2 ON NOT EXISTS (SELECT one FROM foo);
-- empty set

WITH foo AS (
  SELECT 1 AS one
  FROM t0
  WHERE FALSE
)
SELECT t0.c
FROM t0
JOIN t1 ON TRUE
JOIN t2 ON TRUE;
-- 1 row

WITH foo AS (
  SELECT 1 AS one
  FROM t0
  WHERE FALSE
)
SELECT t0.c
FROM t0
JOIN t1 ON NOT EXISTS (SELECT one FROM foo)
JOIN t2 ON NOT EXISTS (SELECT one FROM foo);
-- 1 row

SELECT t0.c
FROM t0
JOIN t1 ON TRUE
JOIN t2 ON TRUE;
-- 1 row
```

### OS:

Linux

### DuckDB Version:

v1.3.2

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

AWS EC2 instance

### Full Name:

Runyuan He

### Affiliation:

The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3(c INT, d VARCHAR);
WITH bar AS (SELECT d FROM t3 WHERE c > 3) SELECT t3.c FROM t3 JOIN t1 ON t3.c = t1.c;
WITH bar AS (SELECT d FROM t3 WHERE c > 3) SELECT t3.c FROM t3 JOIN t1 ON NOT EXISTS (SELECT d FROM bar);
WITH bar AS (SELECT d FROM t3 WHERE c < 1) SELECT t3.c FROM t3 JOIN t1 ON TRUE JOIN t2 ON TRUE;
key_bug_features: MATERIALIZED CTE with FALSE condition in multi-table JOIN
